---
title: "Exploring Australian Frog Data from FrogID"
subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2026 - Final Project"
author: "Kayla Mac"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: images/watercolour_sys02_img34_teacup-ocean.jpg
  data-background-size: stretch
  data-background-opacity: "0.7"
  data-slide-number: none
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:  ['data/customtheming.scss']
    transition: slide
editor: visual
execute:
  warning: false
  echo: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| include: false

#devtools::install_github("mdsumner/ozmaps")

# Load packages here
pacman::p_load(tidymodels,
               tidyverse,
               sf,
               ozmaps,
               ggrepel,
               terra,
               ncdf4,
               scales,
               patchwork)
```

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Plot theme
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11))

# For better figure resolution
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.retina = 3, 
  dpi = 300, 
  fig.width = 6, 
  fig.asp = 0.618 
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

# FrogID data
frogID_data <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-09-02/frogID_data.csv')

# Frog name data
frog_names <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-09-02/frog_names.csv')

# climate data
climate_data <- rast("data/koppen_major_1991-2020.nc")
```

# What is FrogID and Why?

## FrogID

-   Australian frogs are combating habitat loss, disease, and global warming

-   FrogID is an Australian app that allows people to submit frog calls

-   Museum experts use the call and identify the type of frog

-   This information is used to better understand and conserve Australian frogs

# Exploring the Data

## Two Datasets

::: incremental
### FrogID Data

-   Contains when and where the frog call occurred

    -   Includes the date, longitude, latitude, state province

### Frog_names Data

-   Contains the frog's taxonomic status
    -   Includes their scientific names and secondary common names
:::

## Questions

::: incremental
1.  What is the spatiotemporal distribution of Australian frog occurrences, and how does call frequency change across seasons?

    -   **Spatiotemporal:** in relation to both space and time.

2.  Do geographic areas with the highest density of frog occurrences also exhibit the highest levels of taxonomic diversity?

    -   **Taxonomy:** classification of organisms --- different species will determine diversity.
:::

# Code

```{r, echo=FALSE}
oz_states <- ozmaps::ozmap_states
```

## Data wrangling - seasons

```{r season wrangling, echo=TRUE}
frogID_data_wrangled <- frogID_data |>
  mutate(
    event_date_month = month(eventDate, 
                             label = TRUE, 
                             abbr = FALSE) # get the month
  ) |>
  mutate( # label the season by the month
    season = case_when(
      event_date_month %in% c("December", "January", "February") ~ "Summer",
      event_date_month %in% c("March", "April", "May") ~ "Fall",
      event_date_month %in% c("June", "July", "August") ~ "Winter",
      TRUE ~ "Spring"
    ),
    season = fct_relevel(season, 
                         "Spring", "Summer", "Fall", "Winter") # relevel to this order
  )
```

## Data wrangling - climate

```{r climate data wrangling, echo=TRUE}
climate_poly_sf <- as.polygons(climate_data) # convert from SpatRaster to SpatVector
climate_sf <- st_as_sf(climate_poly_sf) # make sf object

climate_sf <- climate_sf |>
  rename(
    climate_zone = stern_dehoedt_2000_major # rename column
  ) |>
  mutate( # change the zones to their climate
    climate_zone = case_when(
      climate_zone == 0 ~ "Equitorial",
      climate_zone == 1 ~ "Tropical",
      climate_zone == 2 ~ "Subtropical",
      climate_zone == 3 ~ "Desert",
      climate_zone == 4 ~ "Grassland",
      climate_zone == 5 ~ "Temperate"
    ),
    climate_zone = fct_relevel(climate_zone,
                               "Equitorial",
                               "Tropical",
                               "Subtropical",
                               "Desert",
                               "Grassland",
                               "Temperate")
  )
```

## Data wrangling - climate + frog calls

```{r}
frog_sf <- frogID_data_wrangled |>
  filter(!is.na(decimalLongitude), !is.na(decimalLatitude)) |>
  st_as_sf(
    coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"),
    crs = st_crs(climate_sf),
    remove = FALSE
  )
```

## Data wrangling - taxonomy

```{r taxonomy data wrangling, echo=TRUE}
frog_names_unique <- frog_names |>
  distinct(scientificName, .keep_all = TRUE)

frogID_data_wrangled2 <- left_join(frogID_data_wrangled, frog_names_unique, 
                                   by = "scientificName") |>
  filter(!is.na(tribe))
```

# Plots

## Australian States and Territories

::: incremental
::: incremental
<div>

Majority of calls seems to occur in the southeastern provinces regardless of the season.

</div>
:::

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
::: column
```{r provinces}
oz_states_plot <- oz_states |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf() +
  geom_label_repel(aes(label = NAME, geometry = geometry), # add state and territories labels
                   stat = "sf_coordinates",
                   min.segment.length = 0,
                   size = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    title = "Australian States and Territories"
  )

print(oz_states_plot)
```
:::

::: column
```{r calls by season}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = oz_states) + # map the states
  geom_point(data = frogID_data_wrangled, # map the frog calls by season
             aes(x = decimalLongitude, y = decimalLatitude, 
                 color = season, shape = season), 
                 alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#0F85A0FF", "#EDD746FF", "#DD4124FF", "#00496FFF")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    title = "Frog Calls in Australia by Season",
    color = "Season",
    shape = "Season"
  ) 
```
:::
:::
:::

## Frog Calls within States and Seasons

```{r fig.width = 16}
frogID_data_wrangled |>
  group_by(stateProvince, season) |> 
  summarise(
    call_count = n(), # determine how many calls per season within each state
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  group_by(season) |> # group by season
  mutate(
    percent_calls = call_count / sum(call_count), # determine the percent of calls
    stateProvince = fct_reorder(stateProvince, percent_calls, .desc = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(stateProvince), !is.na(season)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = stateProvince, 
             y = percent_calls,
             fill = stateProvince)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + # plot percentage
  geom_label(
    aes(label = percent(percent_calls, accuracy = 0.1)), # only to one decimal
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") + # avoid clipping off labels
  facet_wrap(~ season, ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percentage of Frog Calls",
    title = "Percentage of Frog Calls by State, per Season"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16)
  )
```

## Australian Climate Zones

```{r, warning=FALSE}
climate_sf |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = climate_zone), 
          linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = oz_states, # add the Australian states on top of the climate zones
          fill = NA, 
          color = "black",
          linewidth = 0.25,
          linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("tan3", "olivedrab", "olivedrab1", 
               "darkorange", "lightgoldenrod", "lightskyblue")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Australian Climate Zones",
    fill = "Climate Zone",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )
```

## Tribe Distribution

```{r fig.width = 12}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = climate_sf,
          aes(fill = climate_zone)) +
  geom_point(data = frogID_data_wrangled2,
             aes(x = decimalLongitude, y = decimalLatitude),
             alpha = 0.3,
             color = "grey20") +
  facet_wrap(~tribe, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("tan3", "olivedrab", "olivedrab1", 
               "darkorange", "lightgoldenrod", "lightskyblue")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    fill = "Climate Zone",
    title = "Distribution of Tribes Across Climate Zones"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")
  )
```

## Taxonomic Diversity - State

```{r fig.width=12}
frogID_data_wrangled2 |>
  group_by(stateProvince, tribe) |> 
  summarise(
    tribe_count = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  group_by(stateProvince) |> # group by state
  mutate(
    percent_tribes = tribe_count / sum(tribe_count) # percent within each state
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(stateProvince), !is.na(tribe)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = stateProvince, 
             y = percent_tribes,
             fill = tribe)) +                        
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = percent(percent_tribes, accuracy = 0.1)),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 2.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percentage of Frog Calls",
    fill = "Tribe",
    title = "Percentage of Frog Calls by Tribe, within Each State"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16)
  )
```

## Taxonomic Diversity - Climate Zone

# A new section...

## Tables

If you want to generate a table, make sure it is in the HTML format (instead of Markdown or other formats), e.g.,

```{r}
penguins |> 
  head() |>
  kableExtra::kable() |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling()
```

## Images

![Image credit: Danielle Navarro, Percolate.](images/watercolour_sys02_img34_teacup-ocean.png){fig-align="center" width="500"}

# Conclusions

# Q1:

-   Frog calls occur the most in New South Wales during the spring, summer, and fall.
-   In the winter, most calls occur in Victoria followed by New South Wales.
-   In the summer, calls spike in Queensland.

# Q2:

-   

## Feeling adventurous?

-   You are welcomed to use the default styling of the slides. In fact, that's what I expect majority of you will do. You will differentiate yourself with the content of your presentation.

-   But some of you might want to play around with slide styling. Some solutions for this can be found at https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs.
